# seppuku is destructive — defaults to dry-run; --yes performs. We
# can't actually perform the destructive path in a script (it would
# delete the test binary), but every step is covered via dry-run
# output assertions.

# Default (no flag) is dry-run with nothing to remove (no DB exists yet).
exec eon seppuku
stdout 'would remove binary'
stdout 'dry run; nothing was modified'
stdout 'Re-run with --yes to actually perform'

# Add a job so the data dir exists, then dry-run again.
exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name j1 -- echo hi
exec eon seppuku
stdout 'would remove data dir'
stdout 'would remove binary'

# After dry-run, the job is still there (proves nothing was touched).
exec eon show j1 --json
stdout '"name": "j1"'

# --dry-run flag is gone — should now be rejected as an unknown flag.
! exec eon seppuku --dry-run
stderr 'Usage error.*unknown flag'
